error.signature: pip install fails with UnicodeDecodeError in virtualenv with Korean path
error.regex: (UnicodeDecodeError.*pip|pip.*install.*[가-힣]|venv.*encode.*error|pip\ install\ fails\ with\ UnicodeDecodeError\ in\ virtualenv\ with)

verdict.summary: pip install fails when virtualenv is created inside a directory with Korean characters and locale is not UTF-8.

dead_ends.why_fails: Recreating in the same Korean path will reproduce the same encoding error
Cache is not the issue; the path encoding during extraction is the problem

workarounds.action: Create virtualenv in an ASCII path and symlink or set VIRTUAL_ENV
Set PYTHONUTF8=1 and ensure system locale is UTF-8 before creating venv
workarounds.how: export PYTHONUTF8=1 LANG=ko_KR.UTF-8 && python -m venv ./venv && pip install -r requirements.txt
